pr.title: Sync motion callback names with motion enum names
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Renamed or replaced motion callback symbols so their names align with the corresponding motion enum names. The PR body only links to a replaced-symbols gist, and the local slice contains no changed-file list or diff, so the exact symbols and files cannot be verified from this context.
what_changed: Synchronized motion callback names with motion enum names.
The PR body states "Replaced symbols" and links to an external gist, implying a symbol rename/replacement batch rather than behavioral code changes.
No changed files, line counts, or diff excerpt are available in the provided PR slice.
smart_moves: Aligned callback naming with enum naming, reducing the chance that future decomp work has to cross-reference two inconsistent naming schemes.
Kept the change focused on symbol naming consistency, which is useful for repository-wide readability and searchability.
decomp_lessons: When motion callbacks correspond directly to motion enum entries, prefer names that mirror the enum terminology.

Batching related symbol renames can make the symbol table more coherent, but future records should preserve the actual replaced-symbol list in-tree or in the PR dump because external gist-only evidence is fragile.
Name synchronization PRs are useful even without matching changes because consistent identifiers improve navigation and reduce ambiguity during later function matching.
naming_conventions: Motion callback names should stay in sync with motion enum names.
Use enum-derived terminology for related callback symbols when the relationship is known.
searchable_terms: Sync motion callback names with motion enum names
follow_up_queries: Find the replaced-symbols gist linked from PR #820 to recover the exact symbol rename list.
Search later PRs for motion callback names that follow motion enum names to see where this convention was reused.
Inspect commits around 2023-04-28 on branch pr/submotions or master for the actual files changed by this rename-only PR.
